human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel-specific return and fit boundaries, source quality, size and care-label evidence handling, functionality coverage, commercialization notes, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel, textile, and footwear fit-evidence review, exchange availability, replenishment packaging, vendor exception handling, and shortage escalation rather than generic customer support or warehousing.

Authority sources cover FTC care-label, textile-label, and order-fulfillment guidance, California refund-policy law, CPSC apparel safety guidance, GS1 product-data and floor-ready workflows, and ASTM and ISO sizing standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, shortages, vendor blockers, approval-bypass attempts, substitution pressure, incomplete traceability, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, anti-generic boundary enforcement, conflicting-policy escalation, safety-boundary escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/returns-and-fit-specialist/ so it remains distinct from pricing, compliance, safety, procurement approval, fraud, and specialized-fit lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel-textiles-and-footwear returns-and-fit specialist for fit evidence review, exchange availability, vendor exception handling, and shortage-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
